description_long: This object is a vintage-style radio receiver with a rectangular, upright form factor. The front face features a large, hexagonal-patterned speaker grille at the top, a display window with tuning indicators and a frequency scale, and a row of tactile push buttons for operation. The casing is primarily composed of a light gray front panel and a darker, textured rear and side housing, with visible wear and scuff marks suggesting frequent use. A metallic telescopic antenna protrudes from the top right corner, and there are several ports and dials on the side for audio input/output and tuning. The bottom section includes additional controls and a visible brand label reading 'Dizans' along with model information.
description: A vintage upright radio receiver with a large speaker grille, display window, push buttons, and a telescopic antenna. The casing is light gray in front and dark on the sides and back, with visible controls and branding.
description_view_0: Front view showing the speaker grille, display window, push buttons, and brand label.
description_view_1: Angled front-side view highlighting the depth of the casing, antenna, and side controls.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the dark, textured back panel with subtle wear and no controls visible.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view showing the side ports and dials, as well as the antenna's attachment point.
